requirement: Turn the completed OpenAPI contract + ORM schema into a runnable persistence layer: real DB session/dependency plumbing, a contract-honest /ready DB liveness check, a CI migration-vs-models drift gate, and the RecipientIdentifierType enum reconciliation. No feature endpoints (auth/KYC/ledger/entity-gated epics excluded).
backlog: LEDGER (standing, needs a human controller): account/ledger_entry/transaction are structure-only. The posting logic, chart of accounts, entry_type/transaction_type enum values, balance derivation, and the hold formula are HELD on a corrected ledger design (NOT 06's defects). Highest-value next architecture piece and the one requiring a controller, not an LLM pass.
Migration: add a CI autogenerate-consistency check (alembic revision --autogenerate must produce an empty diff vs models).
RecipientIdentifierType: real enum in identity, DeferredEnum in payments/lending — reconcile to one shared enum.

OpenAPI follow-up slice: EP-5 cards (entity-gated), EP-6 lending (entity-gated), EP-7 dividends, EP-8 governance, EP-9 funding, EP-10 round-ups (entity-gated), EP-11 notifications, EP-13 compliance, §3.14 webhooks, §3.12 admin/back-office. Entity-gated epics must be tagged x-entity-gated in the spec, included for record completeness but marked not-buildable.

ORM schema + first Alembic migration from 04's 59 entities — the NEXT architecture task after OpenAPI. Ledger tables must come from the CORRECTED ledger design, NOT encode 06_ledger_addendum's five known defects (esp. the inverted hold formula).

DEC-18 currency APPLY: apply the PO-confirmed derivation table (.softhouse/runs/20260721-currency-policy.json). Anchors: share par ₮10,000 provisional; step-up ₮550k; P2P velocity ₮1.15m; loan min ₮100k / max ₮5.7m; AML monitor ₮11.4m. LINE-BY-LINE by role (polysemy $25 vs $25M). Round-ups/KPIs/$88.20 held. Re-baseline usd after. Decide 06 scope.
Payment rails: 56 ACH/FedNow/SEPA -> Banksuljee (RTGS >MNT 5m) / ACH+ / NETC. Threshold is Governor's-order config, not a literal.
Vendors: Stripe/Plaid/Lithic/Persona leaked into schema field names and error codes — removal is a requirements change.
KPI re-baselining: 100,000 members vs 78,608 sector total; AUM ~1.5x whole sector. Held with the KPI decision.
Rate model: DEC-48 12% base below 17.45% market; DEC-49 6% floor is the subsidised mortgage rate. Both inverted.
verify-docs.sh prose blind spot (04:829, 04:1087) + no context exclusion on n_snake_name.
'etsgiin ner' vs 'etsgiin_ner' spacing — decide canonical spelling.

tasks.title: DB engine + session factory + FastAPI session dependency
tasks.note: branch softhouse/T1-session-plumbing bc5a9c4; independent review APPROVED; merged
tasks.description: Create the SYNC persistence plumbing (SQLAlchemy 2.0 + psycopg3; async is a deliberately-deferred decision — do NOT introduce async here). NEW backend/app/db/session.py: build a module-level lazily-initialised Engine from settings.database_url and a sessionmaker `SessionLocal` (expire_on_commit=False). If database_url is empty (scaffold/dev), the engine stays None and a helper `engine_configured()->bool` returns False — importing the module must NEVER connect or raise. NEW backend/app/api/deps.py: a FastAPI dependency `get_session()` (generator) that yields a Session and closes it in finally; if no DB is configured it raises a clear RuntimeError subclass `DatabaseNotConfigured` (do not map to HTTP here — mapping is the caller's job). NEW backend/app/api/__init__.py (empty package marker). Do NOT import feature models. Do NOT touch main.py (that is T2). ACCEPTANCE: `python -c 'import app.db.session, app.api.deps'` succeeds with DATABASE_URL unset; ruff+mypy(strict) clean on the new files; no float anywhere; no async.

tasks.title: Wire /ready to a real DB liveness check
tasks.note: agent a7495a2bfa6edd016 spawned (worktree), forked from main incl. T1
tasks.description: Fulfil the promise already written in main.py's /ready docstring: check the DB. Depend on T1's get_session/engine_configured. Update the existing /ready handler so it: (a) if no DB configured -> returns {'status':'degraded','db':'unconfigured','currency':MNT} with HTTP 200 (scaffold/dev is a valid state, not an outage); (b) if configured -> runs `SELECT 1` via a session; success -> {'status':'ready','db':'ok',...}; failure -> HTTP 503 {'status':'unready','db':'error',...} (catch, do not leak the exception text). Keep /health untouched. NEW backend/tests/test_ready.py using fastapi.testclient.TestClient: test the unconfigured path (no DATABASE_URL) returns 200 degraded; test the ok path by OVERRIDING the get_session dependency with a fake session whose execute() returns 1 (no real DB); test the error path by overriding to raise. ACCEPTANCE: the 3 tests pass without any real database; ruff+mypy clean; existing 19 tests still pass.

tasks.title: CI check: migration DDL matches ORM metadata (offline)
tasks.note: merged; review APPROVED; micro-fix applied (multiset count guard); gate PASS
tasks.description: Backlog item 1: guarantee the initial migration stays in lockstep with the models WITHOUT a database. NEW backend/app/db/check_migration.py: render the full DDL of Base.metadata offline via sqlalchemy.create_mock_engine('postgresql://', dump) + metadata.create_all(engine) into a list of normalised statements; separately render the migration's intended DDL by importing migrations/versions/0001_initial.py and reading its `_UP` list; normalise both (collapse whitespace, sort statement sets) and assert the CREATE TABLE / CREATE TYPE / ALTER sets are EQUAL; print a unified diff + VERDICT: PASS/FAIL and exit nonzero on mismatch. This is the same mock-engine technique used to generate the migration, now used to police drift. NEW backend/tests/test_migration_matches_models.py wrapping it as a pytest. Wire a step into .github/workflows/ci.yml running `python -m app.db.check_migration` in the ORM-gate job (offline, before the Postgres apply step). ACCEPTANCE: check PASSES on current head; if you mutate a model locally to prove it, it must FAIL — then revert; ruff+mypy clean.

tasks.title: Reconcile RecipientIdentifierType to one shared enum
tasks.note: merged; review APPROVED; owner=deposits.py enum, 2 refs create_type=False; migration regenerated; check_migration PASS
tasks.description: Backlog item 2: RecipientIdentifierType is a real enum in identity.py but DeferredEnum(String) in payments.py and lending.py — one concept, three encodings. Make identity.py the single source of truth: confirm identity.py's RecipientIdentifierType enum is complete and correctly named (name= on the Enum so the Postgres type is shared), then in payments.py and lending.py replace the DeferredEnum column(s) that hold a recipient-identifier-type with `mapped_column(Enum(RecipientIdentifierType, name='recipientidentifiertype'))` imported from app.models.identity. If any of the three actually needs a DIFFERENT value set (verify by reading the columns' meaning in 04), DO NOT force-merge — report the divergence in the handoff instead. Ensure only ONE CREATE TYPE for it (create_type=False on the non-owning references so the migration doesn't double-create). ACCEPTANCE: `python -m app.db.check_models` still PASS; the enum appears exactly once in the rendered migration; all tests pass; mypy clean. NOTE: this changes the schema, so 0001_initial.py must be regenerated to match — if check_migration (T3) exists it will police this; regenerate the migration's affected statements.
